name: probabl-skills
description: Skills for ML experimentation in Python, organized around skrub, scikit-learn, and skore, and more broadly the PyData ecosystem.

skills.title: Build ML Pipeline
skills.summary: Declare the pipeline from data source to predictor as a skrub DataOps graph. Stops at the declared object — no fit, split, tuning, or persistence.

skills.title: Evaluate ML Pipeline
skills.summary: Evaluate a single sklearn-compatible learner: pick the right entry point (skore.evaluate first), the right cross-validator, and consume report metadata.

skills.title: Test ML Pipeline
skills.summary: Router that owns the tests/ folder of an ML workspace and the experiment ↔ test pairing rule. Dispatches to a per-category subskill.

skills.title: Smoke Test ML Pipeline
skills.summary: Diagnostic-by-construction pytest that catches the 'load → featurize → split' anti-pattern by predicting on a disjoint, no-buffer slice of the real data source.

skills.title: Audit ML Pipeline
skills.summary: Owns the audit/ folder: one # %% Python file per experiment that loads its skore report read-only and uses bare-last-expression cells. The agent executes via an in-process IPython runner (scripts/run_audit.py) that streams a markdown digest of each cell's stdout + last-expression repr. Read-only — never calls evaluate or put.

skills.title: Iterate ML Experiment
skills.summary: Drives the iteration loop on top of an ML workspace — owns journal/JOURNAL.md and per-experiment design notes, and dispatches to a sourcing strategy.

skills.title: Iterate from Skore
skills.summary: Source the next experiment by reading the audit digest at scratch/audit/<stem>/audit.md — every 'issue' / 'tip' row drives a Backlog item, following the row's documentation_url for the mitigation.

skills.title: Iterate from User
skills.summary: Source the next experiment from the user directly — free-text, a scientific article URL, or a resource link (GitHub issue / spec / reference repo).

skills.title: Organize ML Workspace
skills.summary: Decide where files live: reusable code, per-experiment scripts (jupytext-style # %%), reports. One file per experiment.

skills.title: Python Code Style
skills.summary: Place the project's ruff.toml template and run ruff (lint + format) on touched files. numpydoc for docstrings.

skills.title: Python Environment Manager
skills.summary: Detect the project's env manager (pixi / uv / poetry / hatch / conda / pip+venv) and issue the right install command. Defaults to pixi when bootstrapping.

skills.title: Data Science Python Stack
skills.summary: Opinionated one-library-per-job Python stack, organized into mandatory / user-choice / optional / transitive tiers.

skills.title: Python API Discovery
skills.summary: Discover the public API of any installed Python package — inspect.signature + pydoc.render_doc for a symbol, dir / pkgutil.iter_modules for a module, versioned-docs WebSearch + cache for narrative.

workflows.title: ML Experimentation Companion
workflows.summary: Companion workflow for the experimentation phase of an ML project: lay out the workspace, build the pipeline as a skrub DataOps graph, evaluate with skore, smoke-test the pipeline topology, audit each experiment's report, and drive a journaled iteration loop. Stops at the experimentation boundary — packaging, serving, and monitoring are out of scope.
